channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Beacontn] Introducing TennSight, Tennessee’s only hub for voter insights
item.description: We hope that TennSight will become the go-to source for you to learn what matters most to your fellow Tennesseans, why it matters, and what can be done about it.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Poll: January 2026 Results
item.description: Today, we released the latest installment of the Beacon Poll, a statewide survey of 1,200 Tennessee voters featuring questions about voters’ views on property taxes, the federal school choice tax credit included in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, and political approval ratings at the state and federal level, and more. Tennesseans continue to prioritize key...

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Op-Ed: Hospitals Collect Tax Breaks. West Virginians Collect Medical Debt.
item.description:
To many West Virginians, healthcare continues to be a burden more than a lifeline as roughly 180,000 residents, or 13.3% of adults, now facing some sort of  medical debt. This is well above the national average of 8.6% and, even worse, nearly one in four of these residents in rural Appalachia have medical debt in […]

The post Op-Ed: Hospitals Collect Tax Breaks. West Virginians Collect Medical Debt. appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Tim Pawlenty: The Crisis Will Subside in Minnesota, but Trust Will Need to be Rebuilt
item.description:
We’ll all be part of that task. Here’s where efforts could be focused. By Tim Pawlenty For the Star Tribune It’s been said that trust is the glue that holds relationships together. That may well explain why recent events in Minnesota have stretched our relationship with government to the breaking point. Fraudsters stole a ton […]

The post Tim Pawlenty: The Crisis Will Subside in Minnesota, but Trust Will Need to be Rebuilt appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Numbers Don’t Lie: The CHOOSE Act is Empowering Alabama’s Families
item.description:
For decades, the Alabama Policy Institute has championed a simple, powerful idea: every child in Alabama deserves a high-quality education, regardless of their zip code or their size of their parents’ bank account. With the passage and implementation of the CHOOSE Act, that vision is finally becoming a reality.

The post The Numbers Don’t Lie: The CHOOSE Act is Empowering Alabama’s Families appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] 312 Out of More than 3 Million Votes
item.description:
Federal prosecutors are closing in on prosecuting more than 100 Minnesotans for rampant fraud in several social service programs designed to serve children and low-income residents. By the time the US Attorney’s office is done investigating, charging, convicting, and sentencing these felons, this unchecked fraud will significantly increase Minnesota’s prison population. But there’s one other […]

The post 312 Out of More than 3 Million Votes appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Rifreedom] TESTIMONY: Senate Commission to Study the Successful Implementation of the Act on Climate
item.description:
In this powerful testimony, Mike Stenhouse, CEO of the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity, explains why energy policy—not taxes, not housing, not government services—is the single most important determinant of Rhode Island’s future prosperity or poverty. Whether one believes in climate change or not, the economic consequences of the Act on Climate are unavoidable.

The post TESTIMONY: Senate Commission to Study the Successful Implementation of the Act on Climate appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Governor Mills’ Supplemental Budget Abandons Fiscal Discipline, Sets Maine Up for Higher Costs
item.description:
Governor Janet Mills’ FY 2026 supplemental budget marks a clear break from the fiscal restraint her administration often claimed to practice. Rather than using Maine’s strong revenue growth to stabilize taxpayers after years of tax increases and inflation, the proposal dramatically expands spending, broadens emergency powers, and doubles down on distortionary tax policy choices that […]

The post Governor Mills’ Supplemental Budget Abandons Fiscal Discipline, Sets Maine Up for Higher Costs appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Affordability tops the list of state priorities for 2026
item.description: The post Affordability tops the list of state priorities for 2026 appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Ayotte: Maximize economic freedom, and New Hampshire will thrive
item.description:
Gov. Kelly Ayotte’s 2026 State of the State address can be summed up in seven words: New Hampshire wins by competing on freedom. Leading with a list of eight No. 1 national rankings for the state—including economic freedom, economic opportunity and taxpayer return on investment—Ayotte noted that, overall, “U.S. News ranks us the #2 state […]

The post Ayotte: Maximize economic freedom, and New Hampshire will thrive appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Senate unanimously passes App Store Accountability Act
item.description:
The App Store Accountability Act passed the Alabama Senate with a unanimous vote on Feb. 5. The bill is focused on online safety for children and teens by requiring parental approval for kids to download apps from the App Store.

The post Alabama Senate unanimously passes App Store Accountability Act appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Senate passes App Store age verification requirement
item.description:
Lawmakers spoke before the Senate in favor of shielding minors from inappropriate content, requiring parental permission for account creation.

The post Alabama Senate passes App Store age verification requirement appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening families for generational change: GCO welcomes Travis Thorne to the team
item.description: The post Strengthening families for generational change: GCO welcomes Travis Thorne to the team appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF talks to KOAT TV about roads
item.description:
SB 2 has passed which means that the road issue has been “put to bed” for the time being. The bill which has been signed by the Gov. includes: $1.5 billion in new road spending via a bond (debt) package. This also includes: A 35% increase in the weight-distance tax on trucks A 25% increase […]

The post RGF talks to KOAT TV about roads appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] An Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature: Stop Advancing a Bill You Know is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Last June, Maine Policy Institute urged Governor Janet Mills to veto LD 1666, a bill expanding ranked-choice voting (RCV) to elections for governor, state senator, and state representative. That request was grounded in clear constitutional text, the Maine Supreme Judicial Court’s 2017 advisory opinion, and Governor Mills’ own legal analysis as Attorney General. Since then, […]

The post An Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature: Stop Advancing a Bill You Know is Unconstitutional appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Metro’s Plan for Making Housing More Expensive
item.description:
By Randal O’Toole High housing prices in the Portland area led to a legislative...

The post Metro’s Plan for Making Housing More Expensive appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Portland’s “Affordable” Housing Averages $800 per Square Foot
item.description:
By Randal O’Toole Note: This post is a sequel to Metro’s Plan for...

The post Portland’s “Affordable” Housing Averages $800 per Square Foot appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Statement of Jim Stergios, Executive Director of Pioneer Institute on Governor Healey’s Opposition to Lowering State Income Tax
item.description:
On Friday, Governor Healey opposed a proposed cut in the personal income tax that would put an average of $1,300 back into people’s pockets. Her claim that it would devastate the budget and gut education is a wild distortion—and it ignores more...

The post Statement of Jim Stergios, Executive Director of Pioneer Institute on Governor Healey’s Opposition to Lowering State Income Tax appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI
item.description:
In recognition of President's Day and George Washington's upcoming birthday, the RI Center for Freedom & Prosperity today launched a video contest encouraging K-12 students to create a video about why they are proud to celebrate America's semiquincentennial, commemorating the 250th Anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

The post Center Launches K-12 Student Video Contest to Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday #America250RI appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] A Roadmap for States to Build High-Quality Online Education
item.description:
BOSTON – State policy is pivotal to the development and availability of online and virtual learning options, and Pioneer Institute’s new Virtual Schools Toolkit provides tactical guidance for developing such policies in the current educational landscape.   “Virtual learning began by serving students...

The post A Roadmap for States to Build High-Quality Online Education appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Legislators look at local zoning rules blocking education, child care & home businesses
item.description:
Local land use regulations do more than severely restrict the size, shape, design and placement of homes, creating scarcity and driving up prices. They also heavily regulate the activities that occur inside those homes. These regulations derive from legitimate local police powers. Local governments exist primarily for the purpose of keeping the peace, and Americans […]

The post Legislators look at local zoning rules blocking education, child care & home businesses appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture
item.description:
West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture Debates over hospital policy in West Virginia usually follow a familiar pattern. Hospital systems warn that their finances are weak. Industry groups say they have too many patients with government insurance. Certificate of Need (CON) rules are defended as needed to keep […]

The post West Virginia’s hospital debate is missing a key piece of the financial picture appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] We Can’t Afford to Trim Your Trees, but We Can Hire Lobbyists
item.description: We’ve long called for reform to the practice of taxpayer-funded lobbying. But this NES response is probably the most glaring example of the need for reform.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Mamdani Gets an Important Tax Fact Wrong
item.description:
At a hearing in Albany last week, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani lobbied state lawmakers to help him balance....

The post Mamdani Gets an Important Tax Fact Wrong appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Challenges Oregon’s Unconstitutional Speech Law in Federal Court
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today presented oral arguments in federal court seeking a preliminary injunction against Oregon HB 3789, a law that threatens the organization with millions in penalties for informing public employees about their constitutional rights.

The post Freedom Foundation Challenges Oregon’s Unconstitutional Speech Law in Federal Court   appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2025 data is in: Four straight years of unaffordable assessed valuation hikes. Where’s the relief?
item.description: Assessed valuation changes for 2025, released this morning, reiterate the need for a constitutional amendment to limit the increases. The average increase for existing residential property (not counting new construction) of 5.9% is more than double the inflation rate, and that’s not the worst of the story. Homeowners in a dozen counties had double-digit increases […]

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law
item.description:
Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law IRG has several outstanding requests that have been pending for months  Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) has waited over eight months for records on DOJ immigration enforcement and the Evers Administration’s Office […]

The post Government in the Shadows: Gov. Evers, AG Kaul, DPI and Other Officials Failing to Comply with Public Records Law appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mspolicy] New Certificate of Need Law – A Small Free Market Win
item.description:
A small win for freedom in Mississippi!  Governor Tate Reeves recently signed HB3, a law that reforms the state’s Certificate of Need (CON) rules.  CON is basically a government approval process healthcare providers (like hospitals) must go through before they can add certain services, build new facilities, buy expensive equipment, or make big expansions. HB3 […]

The post New Certificate of Need Law – A Small Free Market Win appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Crucial Badger-supported housing bill passes through Senate
item.description:
A bill that will ultimately help increase housing supply, make homes more affordable and still allow local municipalities to control where and how fast they grow passed through the Wisconsin Senate this week and was expected to pass in the Assembly Thursday as well.

The post Crucial Badger-supported housing bill passes through Senate appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] The Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox
item.description:
AdministrativeReformToolbox

The post The Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Washington Public Employees Ditch Expensive Union Dues
item.description:
More than 70 union represented University of Washington and state employees, primarily represented by the Washington Federation of State Employees and SEIU 925, canceled their union dues. 

The post Washington Public Employees Ditch Expensive Union Dues appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Hochul continues to bribe Big Health with reckless Medicaid spending
item.description:
For the fourth year in a row, Gov. Kathy Hochul has used the word “unsustainable” to describe her own Medicaid budget. Give her....

The post Hochul continues to bribe Big Health with reckless Medicaid spending appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke Book Review: A History of Money
item.description: by Mark Franke We think of money these days as anything but tangible. Venmo accounts, credit and debit cards, ACH transfers—we don’t really touch “real” money anymore. And so we can’t appreciate its importance in the development of civilization and our comfortable standard of living. “The History of Money: A Story of Humanity” (Henry Holt Read the full article...

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model
item.description:
IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released its “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” on Monday, a practical handbook designed to help other states replicate Wisconsin’s reform framework. WHY IT MATTERS: In the 2010s, Wisconsin led one of the […]

The post IRG Releases “Wisconsin Administrative Reform Toolbox” as Playbook for States Seeking to Replicate Wisconsin’s Model appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity
item.description:
Too many young people still leave Mississippi to chase opportunities elsewhere.  MCPP is on a mission to help change that - by creating the conditions for real, sustained growth so our children and grandchildren choose to stay, build lives, and thrive right here in our state. The good news?  Mississippi is no longer a laggard, but leading. […]

The post Introducing Mississippi Momentum: A Blueprint for Lasting Prosperity appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity
item.description:
Maine lawmakers are currently considering a proposal that would exempt all residential electricity from the state sales tax. While it may be a relatively small cut among all of Maine’s over-taxation and regulation, it is nevertheless good policy as well as an important acknowledgment of the growing reality that electricity costs are putting real pressure […]

The post Ending the Sales Tax on Electricity appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Is state aid to public education growing or shrinking?
item.description:
Is the state contribution to public education funding growing or shrinking?  The complexity of New Hampshire’s school funding formula, and misconceptions about how that formula works, cloud the answer.  But there are a few easy ways to cut through the fog:  State education aid primarily funds students, with some additional aid going to schools. The […]

The post Is state aid to public education growing or shrinking? appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Pelican Institute to Host Louisiana Public Service Commission Candidate Forum at 2026 Solutions Summit
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Media Contact: sydney@pelicaninstitute.org February 24, 2026 Pelican Institute to Host Louisiana Public Service Commission Candidate Forum at 2026 Solutions Summit Baton Rouge, LA – The Pelican Institute for Public Policy, Louisiana’s leading free-market think tank, is proud to announce a special keynote event featuring qualified candidates for the Louisiana Public Service Commission (LPSC) during...

The post Pelican Institute to Host Louisiana Public Service Commission Candidate Forum at 2026 Solutions Summit appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Warns of Lawsuit Against DEI Grant Program in Wisconsin
item.description: The post WILL Warns of Lawsuit Against DEI Grant Program in Wisconsin appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Urges Florida Lawmakers to Pass Bill to Strengthen Union Accountability
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today urged Florida lawmakers to pass SB 1296, legislation that would close a longstanding gap in union accountability by requiring public-sector unions to earn majority support from the employees they seek to represent before being granted exclusive bargaining authority.  

The post Freedom Foundation Urges Florida Lawmakers to Pass Bill to Strengthen Union Accountability appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized
item.description:
As we have done for more than a decade, the Rio Grande Foundation has tracked the votes in New Mexico’s legislative sessions. The 2026 session was a short, 30-day legislative session, but some momentous bills passed (for better AND worse) and some horrible bills that could have passed were defeated, but often included floor votes […]

The post RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Libertas] Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network
item.description:
Libertas Network is growing. As we expand our national programs to serve millions of families and spread the ideas of human flourishing, we need leaders who match the scale of that ambition. That’s why we’re excited to announce the addition of three extraordinary individuals to our board of trustees. Each one brings deep experience, bold […]

The post Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJBIZ: OPINION: Sherrill’s pause on independent contractor rules should stand
item.description:
by Rosemary Becchi February 22, 2026   In one of her first executive orders, Gov. Mikie Sherrill took […]

The post NJBIZ: OPINION: Sherrill’s pause on independent contractor rules should stand first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Beyond the Fiscal Time Bomb: 5 Ways New Jersey Can Cut the Bloat and Empower Families This Year
item.description:
by Erica Jedynak, NJPI Board Member New Jersey is currently sitting on a fiscal time bomb. Despite record-shattering $58 billion […]

The post Beyond the Fiscal Time Bomb: 5 Ways New Jersey Can Cut the Bloat and Empower Families This Year first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] HB 1072 — Voluntary Portable Benefit Plan Act
item.description:
House Bill 1072, authored by Representatives Lee Yancey and Rodney Hall, creates a voluntary “portable benefits” framework for independent contractors and freelancers in Mississippi. The bill allows businesses or hiring parties to contribute money into benefit accounts that are owned by the independent worker, not the company. Those funds can be used for things like health…

The post HB 1072 — Voluntary Portable Benefit Plan Act first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SOLAR, WIND & ARKANSAS MOTOR VEHICLES
item.description: (February 2026) Arkansans are heavily dependent on private transportation. The Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) reports that 93% of Arkansans reach work by private motor vehicles, while only 0.4% use public transportation. The 102,622 miles of roads in Arkansas dwarf the 2,505 miles of freight railroad and 1,860 miles of waterway cited by the BTS, […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] Gaski: The Dems’ Affinity for Illegals
item.description: by John F. Gaski, Ph.D.  The media and American people don’t seem to get it.  A few talking heads have verged on elucidating what is happening, but the true gestalt has yet to be adequately processed in the general media.  Namely, why must today’s liberal Democrats resort to such fanatical measures, in government and in Read the full article...

item.title: [Will Law] Teacher’s Union Lawsuit Attempts to Bankrupt Wisconsin and End School Choice for Families in Need
item.description:
WILL has released a statement in response to a lawsuit filed on behalf of Wisconsin PTA, several Wisconsin school districts, multiple teachers’ unions, individual parents, educators and students, and the Wisconsin Public Education Network, alleging that the Wisconsin Legislature has failed to adequately fund public schools.  

The post Teacher’s Union Lawsuit Attempts to Bankrupt Wisconsin and End School Choice for Families in Need appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: We’ve Lost the Founders’ Mindset SOME OF US have spent decades studying the legislative mind. Most of us have worked through our cynical phase where we assumed it was all selfishness and venal ambition. There are many Indiana legislators sincerely trying to advance measures that they consider important for all of our well-being. It’s just that they aren’t trying Read the full article...

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Lawmakers Move CON Reform Past Key Legislative Deadline
item.description:
Iowa’s Certificate of Need (CON) laws restrict when providers can build, expand, or add services, requiring state approval for major projects and expenditures. The process protects incumbent providers from competition. Recent proposals would significantly narrow CON requirements. Senator Kara Warme’s broad reform bill cleared the first legislative funnel by being passed out of Committee last week. Reform would expand access and reduce costs, especially in rural Iowa. By loosening regulatory barriers and encouraging competition, lawmakers can help providers respond more quickly to the demand for health care services while promoting affordability and innovation.

The post Lawmakers Move CON Reform Past Key Legislative Deadline appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] National group praises Kansas revenue-neutral system, says more must be done
item.description: The American Legislative Exchange Council (ALEC) says the Kansas Truth in Taxation revenue-neutral system is the gold standard of state property tax transparency. ALEC President and Chief Economist Jonathan Williams says, “At the root, unsustainable property tax increases are driven by local government overspending. As property values increase, local taxing authorities pocket the extra revenue […]

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Cutting the Cord: Nebraska Takes the Wheel on Environmental Reviews
item.description:
For years, Nebraska’s major infrastructure projects—such as the long-awaited Lincoln East Beltway—have been held back by a slow-moving hitch: Washington, D.C. Federal red tape and the bureaucratic maze of the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) have historically added years of delays and millions of dollars to local projects.  That changed this week. Governor Jim Pillen...

The post Cutting the Cord: Nebraska Takes the Wheel on Environmental Reviews appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Secretary McMahon Drops the Hammer: Teachers, You Don’t Have to Tap Out to Unions!
item.description:
Earlier this month, U.S. Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon took to social media with an important reminder to teachers: You are not obligated to pay union dues no matter what state you live in. “No employee should feel obligated to hand over their salary to a union that is bankrolling political causes with their...

The post Secretary McMahon Drops the Hammer: Teachers, You Don’t Have to Tap Out to Unions! appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] St. Louis County to Raise Park Fees—That’s Good
item.description:
Listen to this article St. Louis County Executive Sam Page has announced that the county is raising park and recreation fees as part of an effort to address budget shortfalls […]

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Kansas City’s Bus Riders Union Is Right about One Thing
item.description:
Listen to this article Kansas City’s new Bus Riders Union says city hall and the Kansas City Area Transportation Authority (KCATA) need to listen to riders. On that point, it […]

The post Kansas City’s Bus Riders Union Is Right about One Thing appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Tax hikes and smuggling: Will Michigan tempt scofflaws?
item.description: Governor proposes large increase in per-pack excise

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa Roots and Limited Government Principles Shine at President’s Day Event
item.description:
ITR Foundation continues to engage directly with Iowans across the state, participating in a Central Iowa President’s Day event where Policy Director John Hendrickson reflected on the life and principles of native Iowan President Herbert Hoover. Hoover’s Iowa roots shaped his lifelong belief in self-reliance and limited government, from his humanitarian leadership during World War I to his emphasis on civil society and local action over centralized federal control. The event underscores ITR Foundation’s broader mission to educate beyond the Capitol, connecting history, first principles, and public policy while engaging authentically with communities across Iowa.

The post Iowa Roots and Limited Government Principles Shine at President’s Day Event appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Should Reject Price Controls
item.description: Kansas families feel the squeeze. Prescription drugs are expensive. Credit card balances are heavy. Rents and groceries are up. When people are hurting, it’s understandable that lawmakers reach for a simple-sounding fix: cap the price. But that’s where good intentions collide with bad economics. Milton Friedman’s warning applies perfectly here: don’t judge policy by its […]

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Luxury Housing Still Helps Lower-Income Renters
item.description:
Listen to this article In 2019, I argued that Kansas City’s debate over “luxury” apartments missed a basic point: housing markets are connected. When higher-income households move into new buildings, […]

The post Luxury Housing Still Helps Lower-Income Renters appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Progressives Push Trio of ‘Tax-the-Rich’ Bills — Including New State Property Tax
item.description:
Connecticut lawmakers are set to consider three tax proposals at a Feb. 27 public hearing that would increase income taxes, impose a capital gains surcharge, and create a statewide property […]

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Continues to Grow & Expand, Hires New Associate Counsel
item.description:
WILL has announced Kirsten Atanasoff as our new Associate Counsel, expanding our litigation team as we approach new fights to defend property rights, parental rights, and push back against bureaucratic overreach in Wisconsin and beyond.

The post WILL Continues to Grow & Expand, Hires New Associate Counsel appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Ethnic studies courses required to graduate at all 13 four-year UW schools
item.description:
All 13 four-year University of Wisconsin System schools impose an ethnic studies requirement in order to graduate.

The post Ethnic studies courses required to graduate at all 13 four-year UW schools appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.description:
Government-owned broadband networks have a dismal track record. They routinely fail financially, underperform operationally, and saddle taxpayers with...

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Grades now hyper-inflated at UW-Madison
item.description:
The average GPA for undergraduate students at Wisconsin’s flagship university increased to 3.48 in the recently completed fall semester — up from 3.28 just 10 years ago.

item.description:
Arizona lawmakers are advancing what could become the strongest state-level protection in the country against race-based “diversity, equity, and inclusion”..

item.description:
by Andy Mulvihill, NJPI Board Chairman How would you feel if you joined a union and paid $1,400 in dues […]

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Tech companies lead the way with new nuclear contracts
item.description: This new nuclear expansion by Meta and other tech companies sends a message to every major company with large energy needs. The nuclear landscape is different from what it has been in the past.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Childcare Freedom in Nebraska
item.description:
In today’s economy, the high cost and low availability of childcare are major barriers for working families. While policymakers often look toward taxpayer-funded subsidies to solve the problem, recent research suggests a different culprit: state-level overregulation.  Nebraska stands at a critical juncture in this national conversation. According to the 2024 State Childcare Regulations Index from...

The post Childcare Freedom in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Republicans already have a plan for making health care affordable in 2026
item.description:
Affordability is on the lips of lawmakers these days — and with good reason. Nearly half of Americans say they’re struggling to pay for basic necessities like rent, groceries and health care. The good news is that Congress is weighing several reforms that could help make health care in particular more affordable. Many of the […]

The post Republicans already have a plan for making health care affordable in 2026 appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Secures Victory Against Costly, Illegal “Prevailing Wage” Ordinances
item.description:
The Arizona Court of Appeals today sided with the Goldwater Institute in a lawsuit challenging city-wide “prevailing wage” laws in..

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 8
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 8 As another committee deadline approaches, some bills are gaining steam while others quietly stall. In this week’s episode, we unpack a significant school choice–adjacent tax credit bill, a proposed workforce initiative, and a handful of other bills that could either advance or die in the coming days.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 8 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Bonta Sues to Keep Parents in the Dark
item.description:
The lawsuit comes after a federal investigation sparked by a California Justice Center complaint exposing FERPA violations. Just weeks after a federal investigation found that the California Department of Education had “egregiously abused its authority” by hiding students’ gender transitions from parents, California Attorney General Rob Bonta sued to stop federal enforcement actions — the...

The post Bonta Sues to Keep Parents in the Dark first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Who’s Your Daddy?
item.description:
It’s been an amazing week in California news, both for what’s in it as well as what’s not. The Los Angeles Times reported on Sunday that sex-assault claims are so widespread in Los Angeles Unified schools that board members took a break from their liquidation of the bourgeoisie to ask Evil Wall Street bankers to...

The post Who’s Your Daddy? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Lawmaker files anti-student bill right after getting CTU money
item.description:
The bill is based on misinformation and bucks both national trends and public support for a federal program providing donated money to students.

The post Lawmaker files anti-student bill right after getting CTU money appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Week in Review: Fighting a Pinot Power Grab
item.description:
California is well known for its world-class wine industry. Unfortunately, it’s also known for its burdensome business regulations. Now, officials..

item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. The United States Department of Education announced on...

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska Cigarette Tax Increase Would Raise Prices, Reduce Revenue Reliability, and Increase Smuggling
item.description:
Over the past 30 years, Nebraska cigarette consumption has declined from 101 to 27 packs per person per year. This is remarkably good news for public health, but it is bad news for the reliability of cigarette tax revenue. Nebraska raised three times as much cigarette tax revenue in 1986, at 27 cents per pack,...

The post Nebraska Cigarette Tax Increase Would Raise Prices, Reduce Revenue Reliability, and Increase Smuggling appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Many Algorithms Make Light Work: AI Literacy for the Workforce
item.description:
The infrastructure of the United States is adjusting to the realities of artificial intelligence (AI) with data centers, strengthened broadband systems, and galvanized energy grids. Preparing our utilities and systems for the future is essential, but will be futile if the population itself is unequipped. Resources to educate and prepare Americans to participate in the...

The post Many Algorithms Make Light Work: AI Literacy for the Workforce appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Poll: Minnesotans support deportations, blame Walz and Frey for heated rhetoric
item.description:
The recent Thinking Minnesota Poll produced some surprising results as Minnesotans weighed in on immigration policy, ICE operations and attitudes about protests and protesting. The most significant result was the number of people who think the state is on the wrong track. Sixty-five percent of Minnesotans now believe the state is on the wrong track, the largest...

The post Poll: Minnesotans support deportations, blame Walz and Frey for heated rhetoric appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] California’s High-Speed Rail: The 2026 Draft Business Plan Is Ambitious, Expensive, and Still Legally Problematic
item.description:
The California High-Speed Rail Authority released its 2026 Draft Business Plan on February 28, two years after the prior plan and in a radically different environment than when that last plan was written. While quite detailed, the new plan raises serious questions that the Authority has not fully answered: about costs that keep growing, about...

The post California’s High-Speed Rail: The 2026 Draft Business Plan Is Ambitious, Expensive, and Still Legally Problematic first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Spending Watch: Californians Will Pay a High Price for Tom Steyer’s “Split-Role” Proposal
item.description:
Californians Will Pay a High Price for Tom Steyer’s “Split-Role” Proposal Wayne Winegarden March 2026 Gubernatorial candidate Tom Steyer supports “a special election to raise corporate taxes in his first year as governor.” Specifically, he favors removing the Proposition 13 tax limitations for commercial properties – an idea referred to as “spit-roll”. If implemented, split-roll […]

The post Spending Watch: Californians Will Pay a High Price for Tom Steyer’s “Split-Role” Proposal appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] CNN ‘bloodbath imminent’ if takeover goes through
item.description:
Andrew Stiles writes for the Washington Free Beacon about a significant development in cable television news. Paramount is closer than ever to acquiring CNN parent company Warner Bros. after Netflix declined to raise its bid for the media conglomerate. That’s bad news for the failing network’s stable of overpaid hacks, many of whom will be...

The post CNN ‘bloodbath imminent’ if takeover goes through appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan subsidy picks are a short seller’s bonanza
item.description: State officials picked all losers in doomed business subsidy announcement

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Iran didn’t adapt to America’s playbook. Russia and China already have
item.description:
America’s two great-power adversaries, Russia and China, have already drawn significant lessons from Operation Epic Fury. The world now knows that, in a daring daylight strike on a clear Saturday in Tehran, the United States and Israel opened what President Donald Trump, in his address to the nation, called “major combat operations” against the Islamic Republic...

The post Iran didn’t adapt to America’s playbook. Russia and China already have first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Boyfriends are Out? Then Loneliness is In
item.description:
Boyfriends are out. I was surprised to learn this while scrolling through social media. No longer is even the practice of dating safe from the critical eye of those who champion the empowerment of women. British Vogue recently denounced partnership, designating singleness as a “desirable and coveted status,” citing its rise as “another nail in...

The post Boyfriends are Out? Then Loneliness is In first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Praised by Insiders, Sued by Citizens
item.description:
Award-winning revitalization faces legal challenge. Even as Jefferson earns praise for downtown redevelopment, it is defending a lawsuit alleging it improperly moved about $800,000 from its water utility fund to economic development accounts. The dispute centers on process and safeguards. Plaintiffs argue the city failed to prove a lawful surplus, skipped required budget steps, and did not establish repayment — raising concerns about redirecting restricted utility funds. The broader issue is trust and fiscal discipline. Good government is not just visible results; it requires strict adherence to law and protection of core services to maintain public confidence.

The post Praised by Insiders, Sued by Citizens appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Capitol Watch: Structural deficit in state budget persists
item.description:
As we reported in last week’s Capitol Watch, Democrats in St. Paul are attempting to make the 2026 session about Operation Metro Surge and ICE. The numbers from our recent Thinking Minnesota Poll should give them pause. Minnesotans strongly support the mission of the surge and are split on the tactics, despite the onslaught of biased media...

The post Capitol Watch: Structural deficit in state budget persists appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] If Cost of Living Is the Top Concern, Why Are Lawmakers Raising Costs?
item.description:
Connecticut residents have spoken clearly: the cost of living is the top issue facing the state.  According to the University of New Hampshire’s Survey Center, 26% of respondents in the […]

The post If Cost of Living Is the Top Concern, Why Are Lawmakers Raising Costs?  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Phones down, pencils up: How our region addresses phone use in schools
item.description: Growing up in the era of pagers and pay phones, I survived cell phone-free until college. As a mom to a teenage boy, I tried to stave off phone use as long as possible. Debates on appropriate screen time, talks about cyberbullying, and warnings about predators have become the norm. For most students, cell phones are an integral part of everyday life, and thus, their use in school has become a contentious issue. Washington, Idaho, Montana, and Wyoming are each addressing cell phone usage in...

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Assessing Tax Trends in Harris County
item.description:
Does history suggest that property taxes are too high in Harris County? And, if so, have local decision-makers actively helped or hurt the situation? To help answer these questions, let’s review the county’s 2024 Annual Comprehensive Financial Report (ACFR) and gather four types of data—i.e., tax levies, local population, total tax rates, and taxable values—over...

The post Assessing Tax Trends in Harris County first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Republicans didn’t cut Medicaid — but Democrats did make a mess of it
item.description:
Democrats are hoping that healthcare will propel them to victory in this year’s midterm elections. Their central charge is that Republicans have cut Medicaid by $1 trillion over 10 years as part of the One Big Beautiful Bill signed into law by President Donald Trump back in July. There’s just one problem with that claim. […]

The post Republicans didn’t cut Medicaid — but Democrats did make a mess of it appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] 3-2 data dump: Final early voting numbers and looking forward to primary day
item.description:
Cover photo: Official photo of 4th District Congresswoman Valerie Foushee. Foushee is in a competitive primary. North Carolina completed early voting on Saturday, February 28. Here are a few early data points from Vote Tracker and Voter Registration Changes. The end of this post will have Vote Tracker presets for some closely watched races. Voter...

The post 3-2 data dump: Final early voting numbers and looking forward to primary day appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] 7 things to know about Illinois data centers
item.description:
The state’s data center boom is reshaping energy, water use and taxes. Here’s what residents should know about the costs, jobs and incentives involved.

The post 7 things to know about Illinois data centers appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Glahn testifies on electric affordability
item.description:
Minnesota ranks as the #22 highest-priced state for electricity in America. That means most states have lower electricity prices than Minnesota. That’s one of the many facts on energy affordability that I brought before the MN House Energy committee last week. I testified via Zoom and we have posted the first half-hour of my appearance...

The post Glahn testifies on electric affordability appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Empowerms] Empower MS Releases 2025 Impact Report
item.description:
As we look back on 2025, we are reminded how much can change when Mississippians come together around a simple conviction: every person, no matter their ZIP code, income, or background, deserves the opportunity to rise. Last year brought meaningful progress across education, work, and justice. Mississippi continued to earn national attention for its academic…

The post Empower MS Releases 2025 Impact Report first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Voters had no choice in nearly 9-in-10 primary elections
item.description:
Illinois voting data shows voters had no choice of candidate in nearly 9-in-10 Democratic and Republican primaries for state and federal office in 2024.

The post Voters had no choice in nearly 9-in-10 primary elections appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Mass. Officials Talk a Big Game About Democracy. Do They Practice What They Preach?
item.description:
Elected officials in Massachusetts sure have a lot to say about “democracy” these days.   For those paying attention to goings on around here in recent years, hearing politicians trumpeting their state as a citadel defending democracy and the rule of law, you might be...

The post Mass. Officials Talk a Big Game About Democracy. Do They Practice What They Preach?  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Due Date Now: The needed push for maternity price transparency
item.description: For many families, childbirth is the single largest medical expense they will incur. Yet in most cases, parents enter the hospital with little idea what they will ultimately be charged.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Battle of Moores Creek Bridge crushed Loyalist hopes in NC
item.description:
Last Friday was the 250th anniversary of the Battle of Moores Creek Bridge, fought on Feb. 27, 1776.  In addition to writing about that crucial battle last year, the John Locke Foundation’s NC250 project has produced a new film highlighting the importance of that battle in the days leading up to the signing of the...

The post Battle of Moores Creek Bridge crushed Loyalist hopes in NC appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 783 — Health care sharing ministries (+1)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 783 would clarify that money paid to a health care sharing ministry qualifies as an eligible medical expense from an Idaho medical savings account. Rating: +1 Does it directly or indirectly create or increase any taxes, fees, or other assessments? Conversely, does it eliminate or reduce any taxes, fees, or other […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 790 — Certified interior designers (-1)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 790 would establish a voluntary certification for interior designers and grant certified interior designers authority to submit technical submissions to obtain required permits for minor interior alterations to a structure. Rating: -1 NOTE: House Bill 790 is very similar to House Bill 616 (2026), but House Bill 790 includes some additional […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 792 — Sales tax, exemptions, small seller (+1)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 792 would clarify that holding more than two garage sales within a calendar year does not require collecting sales tax unless the seller exceeds $5,000 in total sales.  Rating: +1 Does it directly or indirectly create or increase any taxes, fees, or other assessments? Conversely, does it eliminate or reduce any […]

item.title: [Jamesmadison] A Closer Look at Common Land Use and Housing Terminology: Starters and Nonstarters for Housing Production and Affordability
item.description:

The post A Closer Look at Common Land Use and Housing Terminology: Starters and Nonstarters for Housing Production and Affordability appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] The hidden problem with emergency powers
item.description:
Hawaii’s emergency management law has some critical flaws. Namely, what constitutes an emergency is up to just one person — the governor — who also has wide latitude to address those declared emergencies by indefinitely suspending whatever laws he or she thinks are getting in the state’s way of responding to them. The COVID-19 lockdowns […]

The post The hidden problem with emergency powers appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.
